text:
Ragamuffin Tom lived beneath an old oak tree in the heart of a quiet hamlet. He had made his home in the hollow at the base of the tree where the roots had woven him into the stone. He ate berries from the laurel bushes, drank from the rainwater barrel that his human neighbors had forgotten, and watched the world go by from his snug nook.

Every morning, Tom would wake to the song of birds and the gentle rustling of leaves. He loved the way the dawn light painted everything gold and soft. He lived peacefully, often content as he watched over the fields and the children playing hopscotch.

One day, a stranger came to the hamlet. Their arrival stirred up excitement and curiosity among the villagers. The stranger, a traveling salesman with a tale of wares from faraway lands, had attracted the interest of everyone from the old folks who missed the good old days, to the fast-walking children eager to hear them speak.
